jobs.title: Senior Consultant / Executive Director, Client Strategy
jobs.description:
**Position Summary**

The Senior Consultant / Executive Director, Client Strategy will manage a portfolio of nonprofit clients and help them strengthen fundraising systems, develop major gift strategies, conduct feasibility studies, assess campaign readiness, and execute capital or major gift campaigns.

This role is primarily client service and consulting delivery. The right person will be able to lead client meetings, analyze donor and campaign data, advise nonprofit executives, prepare board\-facing materials, and build fundraising strategy for 7 to 8 figure nonprofit campaigns.

The role will also contribute to PRIDE’s internal growth through occasional content development, training materials, webinars, speaking preparation, SOP development, and firm\-wide strategy projects.

This is a full\-time, work from home, remote role with required regular travel.

**About PRIDE Philanthropy**

PRIDE Philanthropy helps nonprofit organizations build clearer, stronger paths to major gifts, campaign commitments, and long\-term fundraising growth. Our clients are often healthcare, hospice, human service, and community\-based nonprofits that need custom fundraising strategy, stronger donor systems, and hands\-on campaign guidance.

We are hiring a consultant who can manage a portfolio of nonprofit clients, lead high\-quality fundraising strategy work, and represent PRIDE with confidence in front of CEOs, development leaders, boards, and campaign volunteers.

This is a client\-facing role for someone who has strategy\-level fundraising judgment, strong communication skills, and the ability to move work forward without needing constant management.

**Core Responsibilities**

**Client Strategy and Consulting**

* Manage a portfolio of approximately 4–6 full\-service nonprofit clients
* Lead client strategy calls, campaign planning meetings, board sessions, and donor pipeline reviews
* Conduct feasibility studies, opportunity assessments, campaign readiness assessments, and major gift strategy engagements
* Help clients identify, prioritize, and move major gift prospects toward meaningful asks
* Develop practical campaign plans, donor engagement strategies, board engagement plans, and fundraising timelines
* Review client fundraising systems, staffing, board structure, donor data, and revenue performance to identify gaps and opportunities
* Prepare clear recommendations and manage clients week\-to\-week

**Campaign and Major Gift Work**

* Support clients through capital campaigns, comprehensive campaigns, major gift initiatives, and special fundraising projects, typically ranging $3M\-$50M
* Help establish campaign goals, gift charts, prospect lists, cultivation plans, solicitation strategies, and volunteer structures
* Coach CEOs, development staff, board members, and campaign volunteers on donor strategy and next steps
* Ensure client work consistently moves toward measurable fundraising outcomes to report to client leadership

**Client Communication and Relationship Management**

* Maintain consistent, clear, and timely communication with clients
* Set expectations, manage timelines, and keep projects moving
* Prepare agendas, meeting notes, recommendations, and follow\-up materials.
* Build trust with client leadership while still being direct about gaps, risks, and underperformance
* Escalate client concerns internally before they become retention problems

**Internal PRIDE Contribution**

* Contribute to PRIDE’s internal systems, templates, training materials, and standard operating procedures
* Support occasional webinars, educational content, speaking preparation, and client\-facing resources
* Participate in team strategy discussions and help improve service delivery across the firm
* Share useful insights from client work that can improve PRIDE’s methodology, sales process, or consulting model

A successful consultant in this role will:

* Retain a strong portfolio of clients through excellent service and clear results
* Keep clients organized, focused, and moving toward fundraising outcomes
* Build trust quickly with CEOs, development leaders, and board members
* Communicate clearly and consistently, both internally and externally
* Manage multiple clients while maintaining quality of service
* Know when to lead, when to listen, and when to push
* Produce work that is polished enough to go in front of a board or executive team
* Strengthen PRIDE’s reputation with every client interaction

**Required Qualifications**

* Significant experience in nonprofit fundraising, advancement, campaign management, or fundraising consulting.
* Demonstrated experience with major gifts, capital campaigns, campaign planning, donor strategy, or development leadership
* Ability to advise nonprofit executives, development staff, and board members
* Strong writing, presentation, facilitation, and client communication skills
* Ability to manage multiple complex projects at once
* Comfort working remotely and independently
* Willingness and ability to travel regularly for client meetings, board sessions, campaign work, and retreats
* Residence within reasonable distance of a commercial airport
* Access to reliable transportation, a professional remote work setup, strong internet, and a high\-quality video meeting environment

**Preferred Qualifications**

* CFRE credential or willingness to earn/maintain CFRE certification
* Experience working with healthcare, hospice, human service, education, or community\-based nonprofits
* Experience serving as a Development Director, Chief Development Officer, Foundation Executive Director, campaign consultant, or senior advancement leader
* Experience facilitating board meetings, campaign committees, retreats, or donor strategy sessions
* Familiarity with donor databases, prospect pipelines, gift charts, campaign reporting, and fundraising dashboards
* Prior consulting experience or demonstrated ability to manage client relationships independently

**Travel Expectations**

*

jobs.title: Director of Advancement & Engagement
jobs.description:
Part Time Contractor

Position Summary

Friends of Jones (FOJ) seeks a strategic, entrepreneurial, and relationship\-focused Director of Advancement and Engagement to support the long\-term growth and sustainability of Jones College Prep. Reporting to the Fundraising Chair of Friends of Jones and working in close partnership with Jones College Prep leadership, this position will lead advancement initiatives that strengthen relationships with families, alumni, donors, and corporate partners. This is a contract position.

The Director of Advancement will establish and manage constituent engagement, donor stewardship, fundraising support, and corporate partnerships. This role will also provide project management support for initiatives jointly sponsored by the Friends of Jones Fundraising team.

Key Responsibilities

Student, Family, and Alumni Data Management

* Establish processes with Chicago Public Schools and school administration to obtain and maintain contact information for newly accepted and enrolled Jones students and families, consistent with applicable privacy requirements.
* Develop and maintain systems to track student enrollment trends, including students' originating middle schools.
* Create and manage an alumni database, including collection and maintenance of information related to college attendance and majors.

Fundraising and Donor Management

* Collaborate with the Friends of Jones fundraising team to strengthen donor engagement and increase philanthropic support.
* In tandem with Fundraising chair develop the annual fundraising plan. This includes but is not limited to:
* Developing goals for the Flying Eagles Annual Fund
* Develop tactical plan for fundraising – communication plan, events, outreach targets, segmentation approach
* Lead the execution of the tactical plans in conjunction with the fundraising chair and the fundraising team
* Work with the comms team to deliver fundraising messages to the Jones community
* Maintain donor records and oversee donor tracking, stewardship, and recognition programs.
* Work with treasurer to ensure fundraising reports and dashboards are accurate – in line with expectations

Corporate and Community Partnerships

* Develop and launch a corporate outreach and partnership program to connect Jones College Prep with local and national businesses.
* Identify, cultivate, and steward corporate sponsors and strategic partners.
* Create sponsorship opportunities that support academic programs, student experiences, events, and other school priorities.
* Identify other community groups for possible fundraising (i.e. chambers of commerce)
* Work with Friends of Jones leadership to establish goals, strategies, and processes for corporate engagement.

Strategic Projects and School Advancement

* Support strategic communications and marketing initiatives, including potential enhancements to the Jones website and digital presence.
* Coordinate stakeholders, vendors, volunteers, to ensure successful project fundraising execution.

Collaboration and Leadership

* Work closely with Friends of Jones leadership, school administration, volunteers, parents, alumni, and community partners.
* Develop annual advancement goals and measurable outcomes.
* Provide regular updates on progress, challenges, and opportunities.
* Foster a collaborative culture between Friends of Jones and Jones College Prep in support of shared advancement objectives.
* Attend monthly Friends of Jones Board meetings
* Attend weekly fundraising meetings

Alumni Relations and Engagement

* In tandem with alumni coordinator, support ongoing alumni outreach efforts and collaborate with designated alumni leaders.
* Assist in building a culture of lifelong engagement and philanthropy among Jones graduates.

Qualifications

* Bachelor's degree preferred.
* Experience in fundraising, advancement, nonprofit management, alumni relations, communications, education, or a related field.
* Strong project management and organizational skills.
* Experience managing constituent or donor databases and reporting systems.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
* Demonstrated ability to build relationships with diverse stakeholders.
* Ability to work independently while managing multiple priorities.
* Experience with CRM, fundraising, or alumni engagement platforms preferred.

Schedule and Compensation

* Part\-time contract position (approximately 20–25 hours per week; flexible based on organizational needs).
* Some evening and weekend participation will be required for events and meetings.
* Compensation commensurate with experience.

Reporting Structure

The Director of Advancement reports jointly to the President and Fundraising Chair of Friends of Jones and works closely with Jones College Prep leadership to advance shared fundraising, alumni engagement, and strategic development goals.

Pay: $40,000\.00 \- $50,000\.00 per year

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Chicago, IL 60605
